name: Moselle (France)
description: A quartered coat of arms with a small black-and-white shield over the centre: upper hoist red with an armoured arm holding a sword between two yellow roundels; upper fly the Lorraine arms (yellow with a red bend bearing white alerions); lower hoist blue with yellow barbels and crosslets (Bar); lower fly blue and white stripes with a red crowned lion (Luxembourg).
history: The flag quarters the historic arms of the territories that make up the department, including Lorraine, Bar and Luxembourg.
